title: ranunculus macrodactylus
intro: Ranunculus macrodactylus, also known as lesser celandine or pilewort, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the buttercup family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating kidney and liver issues, due to its ability to stimulate the production of bile and improve urine flow. The plant is also valued for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe skin irritations and wounds. Ranunculus macrodactylus can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusion, tincture, or ointment, making it a versatile treatment for various health conditions.
